data.description:
# Surface the ref + source controls on the CLI and MCP tool schemas

## Why

The new `ref` + `source` controls must be reachable by both human (CLI) and agent (MCP)
clients, so a distributed team can target the exact code version a gate runs against.

## What

- CLI: add `--ref <branch|tag|sha>` (default `origin/main`) and `--source {attested|local}`
  flags to the code-reading commands (review / verify-completion / code-review / scan-spec
  surfaces as they exist).
- MCP: add `ref` and `source` arguments to the corresponding tool schemas (`review_plan`,
  `verify_completion`, `review_ticket`, `review_code`, `scan_spec`), with documented defaults
  and outputSchema/text noting `verified_at_sha` in attested results.
- The MCP server no longer depends on its own cwd/branch for the verified code path; document
  that `REBAR_ROOT` only locates the object DB to fetch from.

## Scope

- In: CLI flags, MCP tool-schema args, default wiring, surfacing `verified_at_sha` in results.
- Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4); prose docs (S6).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every code-reading CLI command accepts `--ref` and `--source` with the documented defaults.
- [ ] Every code-reading MCP tool accepts `ref` + `source`; attested results surface `verified_at_sha`.
- [ ] Omitting `--ref` resolves `origin/main` -> SHA (attested) and still signs (pin recorded).
- [ ] `--source=local` is reachable from both surfaces and is reflected as unsigned in the result.
data.title: Surface the ref + source controls on the CLI and MCP tool schemas
